# fix(dataflow): realign staging_environment to real shared infra + implement CREATE/DROP

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `874078d7609a` — fix(dataflow): realign staging_environment to real shared infra + implement CREATE/DROP

**Body**:

test_staging_environment_integration.py targeted a dataflow_test_postgres
container on port 5435 that does not exist in this infra (shared PG is
on 5434). Three @classmethod setup_class blocks with hardcoded
credentials cannot inject the function-scoped test_suite fixture, so
they stayed pointed at the missing container.

Changes:

1. Replace setup_class with @pytest.fixture(autouse=True) per-test setup
   that reads host/port/database/user/password from test_suite.config.
   Class-level state is a misfit once the dep is function-scoped; the
   per-test fixture is the pytest-native way to bind the same attrs.

2. Replace the `port = prod.port + 1` default in
   StagingEnvironmentManager._generate_staging_db_config. Spinning up a
   separate PostgreSQL one port above production is a deployment-time
   concern, not something the SDK should assume. Staging uses the SAME
   server with a different DB name; operators who need a dedicated
   server override on the returned StagingDatabase.

3. Implement _create_staging_database and _drop_staging_database with
   real DDL. They were stubs ("For now, we simulate successful
   creation") — a zero-tolerance Rule 2 violation. Real impl connects
   to the "postgres" maintenance DB (CREATE DATABASE cannot run in a
   transaction and cannot target the DB being created), routes the DB
   name through PostgresDialect.quote_identifier per
   rules/dataflow-identifier-safety.md, and uses DROP DATABASE IF
   EXISTS ... WITH (FORCE) to kick leftover sessions.

4. Pre-create `test_table` with 10 rows in
   test_data_sampling_simulation so the real COUNT(*) + sampling SQL
   has data to work against. Tier 2 rules require real infrastructure;
   the original test depended on the table being seeded in the
   dataflow_test container.

Result: 12F/1P -> 0F/13P on test_staging_environment_integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
